assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that spot VMs can be preempted anytime and discusses how this affects upgrades, including that workloads should already be fault-tolerant

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response states 'Spot workloads should already be fault-tolerant (spot can be preempted anytime)' and explains this is beneficial for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends the opposite - conservative surge settings with maxSurge=1, citing spot availability concerns rather than leveraging fault tolerance

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends maxUnavailable=0 in the conservative surge strategy, contradicting this expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response extensively discusses spot availability issues, stating 'If spot instances can't be provisioned for surge nodes, the upgrade will stall'

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'PDBs configured but not overly restrictive' in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands provided for configuring surge settings, including specific --max-surge-upgrade and --max-unavailable-upgrade parameters

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response does not suggest prioritizing spot pool upgrades or upgrading them before on-demand pools; instead treats mixed pools as single units
